transcript.text: No one's being honest and teaching
founders this. You're young. You're at
the Kleiner Perkins holiday party. You
have all the venture capitalists in the
world and all the CEOs. You're at your
first company. Maybe you're from a
different country. You're like, "Oh my
god, this is my shot. I have all these
people I need to talk to and I need to
convince this person of that and make
this connection." It becomes very
transactional. If you show up like a
little Energizer Bunny, you're going to
scare off. You're going to project
totally the wrong vibe. understanding
how to show up, take a beat, come in
with a a mindset not of scarcity, but of
abundance. Understand how to give more
than you take. Understand how to build a
relationship, not collect business
cards. Like, these are things that
actually serve you massively well. The
product does matter a lot, but
understanding these rules can be the
difference between doing really well and
missing a business deal.
